concerns.analysis: The image manifest indicates that almost all images have '"valid": false' due to a missing dependency error ('No module named 'PIL''). This means the base64 data for these images is corrupt or truncated, and even if an tag is present, they will not render properly. This affects the agency logo on Page 1 and meaningful maps on Pages 16, 35, 36, and 37. Furthermore, several critical figures from the source PDF (Page 17: Site Map, Page 18: Soil Contaminant Concentration Map, Page 19: Groundwa
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Figures 2 through 6 on pages 17-21 display complex site maps with contaminant contours, monitoring well locations, and boundary lines.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML lacks tags for Figures 2 through 6, and the images listed in the manifest (such as the map on page 16 and the logo on page 1) are flagged as invalid/corrupt and will fail to render.

concerns.description: Fidelity Content evaluation failed — dimension not assessed, requires human review
concerns.analysis: The content fidelity call did not produce valid results.

concerns.description: Fidelity Structural evaluation failed — dimension not assessed, requires human review
concerns.analysis: The structural fidelity call did not produce valid results.

concerns.description: Table caption coded as header row: 16 table(s) have a single spanning first row that is likely a caption/title
concerns.analysis: caption_as_header=16. Examples: p17: "SITE MAP"; p18: "SB-10 (12/13/17)"; p18: "SB-05 (1/27/16)". First row spans all columns — should be a <caption> element, not a header row.

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 56 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=56, examples=['https://harthick.sharepoint.com/sites/MasterFiles-1/Shared D', 'http://nwi.fws.gov/', 'https://fws.maps.arcgis.com/home/webmap/print.html']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (109): i; ii; https://harthick.sharepoint.com/sites/MasterFiles-1/Shared D
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=109. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: 5 bare URL(s) in text not converted to hyperlinks: http://www.fws.gov/birds/management/managed-species/birds-of-conservation-concer; http://www.fws.gov/birds/management/project-assessment-tools-and-guidance/conser; http://www.fws.gov/migratorybirds/pdf/management/nationwidestandardconservationm; jay.king@ncdenr.gov; http://portal.ncdenr.org/web/wm/DSCA/PublicNotices.
concerns.analysis: bare_url_count=5. URLs appear as plain text instead of clickable links.

concerns.description: Table cells contain 2 unlinked URL(s)/email(s) that should be hyperlinks
concerns.analysis: bare_urls_in_tables=2. Examples: p1: www.harthickman.com; p43: https://ecos.fws.gov/ecp/species/2212. URLs and emails in table cells should be clickable links.

concerns.description: List numbering restart(s): 9 ordered list(s) restart numbering at 1 instead of continuing sequence
concerns.analysis: restart_count=9. Examples: p28: 4 single-item ordered lists (fragmented); p31: 7 single-item ordered lists (fragmented). Ordered lists that restart numbering suggest content was split across page breaks and not reassembled.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 4 table(s) have a data row marked as header: "Appendix A", "TITLE", "TITLE"
concerns.analysis: data_in_th=0, datarow_as_header=4. Tables use <th> for data values (misrepresenting data as headers) or have a data row mismarked as a header row. Screen readers rely on <th> to announce column/row context.

concerns.description: Form elements detected: 10 form(s) with 25 field(s) — forms lose functionality in HTML
concerns.analysis: form_count=10, total_fields=25. PDF form fields (checkboxes, text inputs, signatures) cannot be faithfully rendered as static HTML.

concerns.description: Links displaced to end of page: 19 link(s) clustered at bottom of 5 page(s) instead of inline in text
concerns.analysis: displaced_link_pages=5, total_displaced=19. Links appear as standalone elements at the end of pages rather than being inline in the paragraph text.

concerns.description: Repeated footer/header text (2x): "dsca site id dc790003"
concerns.analysis: Short text with footer-like pattern appears on 2 pages
Short text with footer-like pattern appears on 2 pages

concerns.description: Repeated footer/header text (2x): "if you have any questions, please feel free to contact me at"
concerns.analysis: Short text with footer-like pattern appears on 2 pages

concerns.description: Mixed numbering types at same list level (1 list(s)): p55:letter+roman — possible sub-point flattening
concerns.analysis: flat_lists=1. Lists with mixed numbering types (numeric+letter or numeric+roman) at the same depth level suggest sub-points were flattened.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 17 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th_time=0, no_th_tables=17. Minor semantic markup issues: tables lack <th> elements or use time values as headers. These affect screen reader context but are less severe than data misrepresentation.

concerns.description: Source PDF contains 31 instance(s) of blue-styled text without hyperlink annotations. These appear to be intended as hyperlinks but were not annotated in the PDF (likely lost during Word-to-PDF export). This is a source document limitation, not a pipeline defect. Examples: page 25: "mg/L"; page 25: "MW-03"; page 25: "MW-04"; page 25: "27-Jan-16"; page 25: "0.00531"
concerns.analysis: orphaned_blue_text_count=31. Blue text without link annotations detected via PyMuPDF color extraction.
